mnemonics: PDA: P for Push, D for Down, A for Automata (a stack that pushes down)
Turing Machines: Think 'Tape & Transition Table' for ultimate flexibility
keyPoints: A language is recursively enumerable (recognizable) if a Turing Machine accepts all its strings.
A language is recursive (decidable) if a Turing Machine halts and accepts all its strings and halts and rejects all non-members.
The Church-Turing Thesis states that any computable function can be computed by a Turing Machine.
commonMistakes: Assuming all languages are decidable; many important problems are undecidable (e.g., Halting Problem).
Confusing language recognition (TM may loop) with language decidability (TM always halts).
